name: Brown Rice
summary: Whole-grain rice that keeps the fiber-, magnesium-, and B-vitamin-rich bran that white rice has milled away.

benefits.claim: A whole grain linked to lower disease risk
benefits.mechanism: Brown rice retains the bran and germ — and with them the fiber, minerals, and plant compounds that milling strips out to make white rice. Higher whole-grain intake is consistently associated with lower risk of heart disease, type 2 diabetes, and premature death.

citations.title: Carbohydrate quality and human health: a series of systematic reviews and meta-analyses (Reynolds et al., Lancet 2019)

benefits.mechanism: The bran that brown rice keeps is a good source of magnesium — a mineral involved in hundreds of enzyme reactions, in muscle and nerve function, and in bone — much of which is lost when rice is milled to white.

citations.title: Magnesium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: White and brown rice start as the same grain — white is simply brown rice with the bran and germ polished off, which removes most of its fiber, magnesium, and B-vitamins.
